title: Ordinance Updates and Timelines
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: February 24, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Lance Ferrato, Director of Licensing and Code Enforcement
Ashley Turney, Assistant City Manager
DEPT: Licensing and Code Enforcement Department
SUBJECT: Ordinance Updates and Timelines
This memorandum provides an update of the Licensing and Code Enforcement Department's efforts to update several ordinances at Council's direction. The topics below are listed in order of tentative appearance before Council.
Nonprofit Organization Registration – Chapter 4.04 (Business Licensing)
Date Initiated by Council: June 11, 2025

Background: Historically, the registration and/or licensure of nonprofit organizations (NPOs) has been a topic of discussion in terms of city planning and public safety. Since NPOs are exempt from licensing, the Planning Division may not always have knowledge of an NPO's existence and could potentially approve an incompatible use nearby. Similarly, applicable City staff is not always aware of the existence of NPOs so NPOs aren't necessarily inspected and/or reviewed like a commercial business would be. For these reasons, Council requested that the topic of nonprofit organization registration be researched and outreach conducted.

Council Direction: Conduct community outreach and return with a draft ordinance regulating the registration of nonprofit organizations.

Current Status: Licensing and Code Enforcement Department staff has held three stakeholder meetings (November 17, 18, and 20, 2025) and collected data through a survey. At this time, Licensing and Code Enforcement Department staff continues to organize data and stakeholder feedback to present to Council to get further direction on the nonprofit organization registration item.
Tentative Return to Council: March 11, 2026


Property Maintenance Code Update – Chapter 14.05 (Code Enforcement)
Date Initiated by Council: December 3, 2025

Background: The City's current property maintenance code was last updated in 2012 (2012 edition); however, the International Code Council has updated the International Property Maintenance Code four times since then (2015, 2018, 2021 and 2024). Furthermore, in 2014, Council adopted an additional section to supplement the International Property Maintenance Code specifically addressing downtown property maintenance standards, which also has remained in the additional section's original form since adoption.

Council Direction: Return with an ordinance introduction amending RMC Chapter 14.05 to reflect the 2024 edition of the International Property Maintenance Code including any regional modifications as discussed.

Current Status: Licensing and Code Enforcement Department staff continues to research best practices and meet with industry experts to determine the best options to include in the next adoption of the property maintenance code.

Tentative Return to Council: April 2026

Alcoholic Beverages – Chapter 5.07 (Business Licensing)

Date Initiated by Council: March 29, 2023

Background: The majority of Titles 4 and 5 were last revised in July of 1997. Since then, chapters and sections have been adopted, repealed, and amended, but the general framework of Titles 4 and 5 has remained intact. After engaging with a consultant and preparing to bring a draft forward it was determined that, based upon community feedback, Licensing and Code Enforcement Department staff would step back and reevaluate a more effective strategy to get public input. Since then, Licensing and Code Enforcement Department staff has been analyzing chapters individually to ensure full transparency with the business community.

Council Direction: Open and amend Titles 4 and 5 in Titles 4 and 5's entirety.

Current Status: In late 2024, Licensing and Code Enforcement Department staff began meeting weekly with a group of alcohol-based business owners and industry representatives to dissect Chapter 5.07 in Chapter 5.07's entirety. Chapter 5.07 not only deals in alcohol regulation, but also live entertainment, which has been a topic of discussion with the Planning Division over this same timeframe. As the entertainment discussion continues, Licensing and Code Enforcement Department staff will continue to craft ordinance language and circle back with alcohol license stakeholders before returning to Council to discuss options.

Tentative Return to Council: May 2026

fields.authors: Lance Ferrato, Director of Licensing and Code Enforcement; Ashley Turney, Assistant City Manager
fields.tldr: The Licensing and Code Enforcement Department provides a status update on three Council-directed ordinance efforts: nonprofit organization registration, property maintenance code modernization, and alcoholic beverages/entertainment regulation. Nonprofit registration stakeholder outreach is complete and will return to Council in March 2026. The property maintenance code update is under research and targets April 2026. The alcohol/entertainment chapter overhaul is in ongoing stakeholder meetings and targets May 2026.
fields.key_stats: Property maintenance code last updated in 2012; International Property Maintenance Code updated 4 times since then (2015, 2018, 2021, 2024). Titles 4 and 5 general framework last revised July 1997. Three NPO stakeholder meetings held November 17, 18, and 20, 2025.
fields.key_decisions: Council previously directed: (1) research and draft ordinance for nonprofit organization registration; (2) amend RMC Chapter 14.05 to reflect 2024 International Property Maintenance Code; (3) open and amend Titles 4 and 5 in their entirety.
fields.action_items: Return NPO registration item to Council by March 11, 2026 with stakeholder data and further direction request; return Property Maintenance Code ordinance introduction in April 2026; return Alcoholic Beverages/Chapter 5.07 options to Council in May 2026.

dates: December 3, 2025 (Property Maintenance Code item initiated by Council)
April 2026 (tentative Property Maintenance Code return to Council)
March 29, 2023 (Alcoholic Beverages item initiated by Council)
May 2026 (tentative Alcoholic Beverages return to Council)
people: Lance Ferrato, Director of Licensing and Code Enforcement
